claims.claim_text: SPACE stands for Satisfaction and well-being, Performance, Activity, Communication and collaboration, and Efficiency and flow.
claims.rationale: The SPACE paper names and defines each dimension directly.

claims.claim_text: The SPACE framework was created to move organizations away from one-dimensional productivity measurement.
claims.rationale: The paper repeatedly argues that productivity cannot be reduced to a single metric and presents SPACE as the alternative.

claims.claim_text: Google's Project Aristotle research helped popularize psychological safety as a key contributor to effective teams.
claims.rationale: The primary source is Google's own re:Work material on Project Aristotle, but that host is outside the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Developer portals and internal developer platforms often fail when they are launched as catalogs of tools rather than designed as task-oriented workflows that help developers complete real jobs.
claims.anchor_text: Developer portals and internal developer platforms often fail when they are launched as catalogs of tools rather than designed as task-oriented workflows that help developers complete real jobs.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Developer portals and internal developer platforms are more useful when they are designed around real developer tasks, not just as catalogs of tools.
claims.rationale: Allowlisted sources support the task-oriented design principle, but not the broader empirical claim that such platforms 'often fail' in exactly this way.

further_reading.title: The SPACE of Developer Productivity
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary source for SPACE and for the warning against single-metric productivity measurement.

notes: No claims were seeded from quiz items, exercise scaffolds, diagrams, code blocks, or pedagogical examples. Tables were not seeded unless they introduced named external frameworks.
